file_insights.purpose: Comprehensive Android development environment setup guide
structure: Table of contents with internal links

key_content.design_pattern: Tiered requirements with detailed notes and practical guidance

patterns_discovered.description: Hardware specs documented at three levels (minimum/recommended/optimal) with rationale for each tier
patterns_discovered.example: RAM: 8GB minimum, 16GB recommended, 32GB optimal with explanations for performance trade-offs

patterns_discovered.description: OS requirements differentiated by platform with specific version numbers and architecture notes
patterns_discovered.example: Different hardware acceleration methods for Windows (Intel/AMD), macOS (Hypervisor Framework), Linux (KVM)

patterns_discovered.description: Documentation includes real-world warnings alongside official specs
patterns_discovered.example: 'Note on RAM' section clarifies that 8GB official minimum is insufficient for practical development

patterns_discovered.description: Main sections planned across multiple subtasks with clear dependencies and progression
patterns_discovered.example: Subtask 2-1 covers prerequisites; 2-2 through 2-6 marked for future completion

gotchas_discovered.detail: 8GB official minimum leaves only 4GB for IDE/OS after emulator claims 4GB; 16GB actually required
gotchas_discovered.impact: Developers with 8GB may experience significant slowdowns

gotchas_discovered.detail: Linux requires 32-bit library support for SDK; Windows has different acceleration for Intel vs AMD
gotchas_discovered.impact: Setup process varies significantly by OS

gotchas_discovered.detail: SSD provides 3-5x faster builds than HDD but not highlighted as critical until 'Recommended' tier
gotchas_discovered.impact: Users with HDD may assume compatibility when practical performance is poor

gotchas_discovered.detail: 32-bit Windows explicitly not supported but not emphasized in overview
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential confusion for older Windows systems

gotchas_discovered.description: Different methods required per OS/CPU combination (HAXM/Hypervisor/KVM) with no single solution

approach_outcome.deliverable: Complete SETUP.md with comprehensive prerequisites section
approach_outcome.quality_assessment: High - detailed, well-organized, includes version specifics and practical guidance
approach_outcome.documentation_maturity: Foundation established; prerequisites section provides solid baseline for subsequent sections

recommendations.recommendation: Maintain the three-tier (minimum/recommended/optimal) requirement structure in subsequent sections for consistency
When completing sections 2-2 through 2-6, update placeholders and add forward/backward links between sections
Consider adding platform tabs or separate subsections in future sections (Android SDK, Studio Setup) to avoid duplication of platform-specific instructions
Section 2-6 (Troubleshooting) should directly reference specific version numbers and configuration details mentioned in prerequisites
Consider adding comparison tables for OS requirements and hardware tiers to improve scannability in future revisions
Continue emphasizing real-world caveats (like RAM vs official specs) in future sections; this pattern is effective for developer experience
